sections.paragraph:
εὐνουχία, ας, ἡ (s. two next entries; so far found only in Christian wr., e.g. Athenagoras, Legat. 33) state of being unmarried Agr 18 (=Clem. Al., Strom. 3, 15, 97).—DELG s.v. εὐνή.

  1. wr. = writers, writings

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

Agr Agr = Agraphon (non-canonical sayings of Jesus)—List 1

Clem. Clem. Al. = Clement of Alexandria, II–III a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 409
